body_txt: Throughout history , cats have gone paw-in-paw with the supernatural. In Ancient Egypt they were considered to be sacred to the goddess Bastet; in medieval Europe they were tied to witchcraft ; in Japan and Russia they are heralds of good luck; in the Muslim world , they are regarded as Born Lucky , benevolent jinn , while in other places they are harbingers of misfortune . In Western culture, cats are purported to have nine lives , and black cats in particular carry associations with witchcraft and both bad and good luck . Unsurprisingly, when people write about cats in works with magic, cats tend to have magic too. Because All Witches Have Cats , cats are almost certain to make an appearance in a work with classic witches , and more often than not, these felines will possess unusual abilities. Even in media without witchcraft, cats alone will sometimes be able to perform supernatural feats while other animals will be extremely mundane . If any one animal has the ability to be understood by humans or to use human speech fluently , chances are that it's a cat. A person will often choose the form of a cat when using magic to disguise him or herself as an animal . Similarly, using transformation as a form of punishment often results in a feline transformation . Sometimes their magic might appear in a Follow the White Rabbit form. Usually a magical cat will be intelligent , but even non-sapient cats can have magical powers. A common depiction gives them a form of Evil-Detecting Dog abilities by letting them perceive things that humans can't, such as seeing invisible beings , fairies, and spirits, as an extension of the keen senses of natural cats and of their reputation for occasionally startling or staring at seemingly nothing. Wittingly or not, cats may even be using their abilities to keep up the Masquerade . Despite all this, cats are not often Bond Creatures , except maybe to witches in the fluffiest of works, due to their reputations for being aloof and independent. This trope may mean a cat is an Evil Sorcerer if used in a story where Cats Are Mean . The mystical associations can be partially explained by cats' general behavior, especially by how they compare to their great domestic frenemies, dogs. Even without entering the nebulous field of ethology, cats tend to have much more subtle emotional cues than dogs have, and their body language is distinctly less humanlike (for instance, dogs can smile the same basic way we humans do, while cats usually only "smile" by narrowing their eyes). Furthermore, their faces are dominated by their penetrating, slit-pupiled eyes, producing the impression of a remote, mysterious intelligence. Cats also hang out in the weirdest places, can walk around silently thanks to their paw pads, and are infamous for staring at the space in front of them or at a wall and meowing, all of which conspires to appear that they live between our reality and... some other . Cats also have superior sight and hearing compared to humans thanks to them being both predator and prey, so it can be said they do hear and see things we cannot. Super-Trope to Fairy Feline . See also Black Cat Luck , Cats Are Superior , Cats Have Nine Lives , Familiar , Chariot Pulled by Cats , Maneki Neko , Bakeneko and Nekomata , Asian Lion Dogs , and Rabbit Magician . The direct opposite of this trope is Robot Dog .
